entries.definition:
كتاب الباء

بصط - بصع

(بصر) الباء والصاد والراء أصلان: احدهما العِلْمُ بالشيء؛ يقال هو بَصِيرٌ به. ومن هذه البَصيرةُ، والقِطعةُ من الدّمِ إذا وقعت بالأرض استدارت. قال الأشعر:

b-s-r eyesight, to see; to comprehend, to realize; proof, sign,
eye opener; to warn, to guide; to reflect, to ponder. Of this root,
14 forms occur 148 times in the Qur’an: بَصُرَ baṣura three times;
يُبَصَّر yubassar once; أَبْصَرَ ʾabṣara 27 times; أَبْصِر ʾabṣir twice;
بَصِير baṣīr 51 times; بَصِيرَة baṣīratun twice; بَصَائِر baṣāʾir five
times; تَبْصِرَة tabṣiratun once; مُبْصِر mubṣir three times; مُبْصِرُون
mubṣirūn once; مُبْصِرَة mubṣiratun three times; مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ
mustabṣirīn once; بَصَر baṣar 10 times and أَبْصَار ʾabṣār 38 times.

بَصُرَ baṣura u [v. intrans. with prep. بِ] 1 to see, comprehend,
to gain knowledge of something, (20:96) قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يُبْصِرُوا بِهِ
he said, ‘I saw what they did not see’ [in another interpretation: ‘I
have gained insight into something which they were unable to
entries.definition_english:
b-s-r eyesight, to see; to comprehend, to realize; proof, sign,
eye opener; to warn, to guide; to reflect, to ponder. Of this root,
14 forms occur 148 times in the Qur’an: بَصُرَ baṣura three times;
يُبَصَّر yubassar once; أَبْصَرَ ʾabṣara 27 times; أَبْصِر ʾabṣir twice;
بَصِير baṣīr 51 times; بَصِيرَة baṣīratun twice; بَصَائِر baṣāʾir five
times; تَبْصِرَة tabṣiratun once; مُبْصِر mubṣir three times; مُبْصِرُون
mubṣirūn once; مُبْصِرَة mubṣiratun three times; مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ
mustabṣirīn once; بَصَر baṣar 10 times and أَبْصَار ʾabṣār 38 times.

بَصُرَ baṣura u [v. intrans. with prep. بِ] 1 to see, comprehend,
to gain knowledge of something, (20:96) قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يُبْصِرُوا بِهِ
he said, ‘I saw what they did not see’ [in another interpretation: ‘I
have gained insight into something which they were unable to

entries.definition:
95
see’] 2 to keep an eye on, to watch over (28:11) وَقَالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِ قُصِّيهِ
فَبَصُرَتْ بِهِ عَنْ جُنُبٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ and she said to his sister, ‘Track
him,’ so she watched him from a distance, while they were
unaware.

يُبَصَّر yubassar [imperf. of pass. v. II بُصِّرَ buṣṣira] to be made to
see/comprehend something, to be given an insight into (70:11)
يُبَصَّرُونَهُمْ they are enabled to see them (or, they are given an
insight into them).

أَبْصَرَ ʾabṣara [v. IV trans.] 1 to see (7:179) وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ لَا
يُبْصِرُونَ بِهَا they have eyes they do not see with 2 to comprehend, to
understand (28:72) أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ do you not comprehend? 3 to find
guidance (6:104) قَدْ جَاءَكُمْ بَصَائِرُ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ فَلِنَفْسِهِ clear
proofs have come to you from your Lord—so whoever finds
guidance, it is for himself.

أَبْصِرْ بِ ʾabṣir bi [exclamation] how strong the eyesight is!
(18:26) أَبْصِرْ بِهِ وَأَسْمِعْ say, ‘God knows best how long they remained,’
His is all that is hidden in the heavens and the earth—how well
He sees!, how well He hears!

بَصِير baṣīr [intens. act. part./quasi-act. part.] 1 one who can see,
one endowed with sight (12:96) فَارْتَدَّ بَصِيرًا then, when the bearer
of good news came and placed it onto his face—thereupon he
became able to see again 2 one discerning, endowed with
insight *(42:11) البَصِير [attribute of God] the All-Seeing, the All-Discerning.

بَصِيرَة baṣīratun I [v. n./n., pl. بَصَائِر baṣāʾir] clear evidence,
sure knowledge (12:108) أَدْعُو إِلَى اللَّهِ عَلَى بَصِيرَةٍ I call to God with
sure knowledge II [n.] witness, overseer (75:14) بَلِ الْإِنْسَانُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ
بَصِيرَةٌ truly, man is a [clear] witness against himself [in another
interpretation: there is an observer observing him].

تَبْصِرَة tabṣiratun [v. n.] providing insight, showing the way,
eyesight (50:8) تَبْصِرَةً وَذِكْرَى لِكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُنِيبٍ as an eye-opener and a
reminder for every servant who turns to God.

96                                    ب/ص/ل

مُبْصِر mubṣir [act. part.; pl. مُبْصِرُون mubṣirūn; fem.
مُبْصِرَة mubṣiratun] 1 one who sees; one who has insight, one who
discerns (7:201) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا إِذَا مَسَّهُمْ طَائِفٌ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ تَذَكَّرُوا فَإِذَا هُمْ
مُبْصِرُونَ those who are mindful of God, if a visitation [of thoughts]
from Satan touched them, they remember [Him] and, lo and
behold, they have insight 2 light/sight-giving (10:67) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ
اللَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَالنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا He it is who made the night for you to
rest in [it] and the day giving light/sight 3 clear, evident, plain to
see (27:13) فَلَمَّا جَاءَتْهُمْ آيَاتُنَا مُبْصِرَةً but when Our clear signs came to
them.

مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ mustabṣirīn [pl. of act. part. مُسْتَبْصِر mustabṣir] one
who seeks insights, capable of having insight into matters (29:38)
وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ وَكَانُوا مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ Satan made
their foul deeds seem alluring to them and barred them from the
right way, [even] though they were capable of seeing.

بَصَر baṣar [n., pl. أَبْصَار ʾabṣār] eyesight, eyes, sight, vision,
sense of sight (6:103) لَا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصَارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصَارَ vision cannot
encompass Him, but He encompasses all visions.

see’] 2 to keep an eye on, to watch over (28:11) وَقَالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِ قُصِّيهِ

96                                    ب/ص/ل

مُبْصِر mubṣir [act. part.; pl. مُبْصِرُون mubṣirūn; fem.
